message:
chore(beads,fr-conformance): file frankenredis-aqgx + rustfmt the RDB corpus harness

Two related cleanups for the live RDB corpus test landed in
eeea0ae and narrowed in f3dfe89:

  1. Open frankenredis-aqgx ("[HIGH] fr-persist::decode_rdb:
     support upstream compact encoding type tags 11/16/17/18/20").
     This is the bead that owns lifting the threshold pins this
     test set to 0 in f3dfe89. Match table for the missing decoder
     paths is captured in the bead description so the next agent
     can wire fr-store's existing listpack/intset/quicklist
     decoders into fr-persist::decode_rdb without having to rebuild
     the spec from scratch:
       * RDB_TYPE_SET_INTSET (11) — all-integer set, intset payload
       * RDB_TYPE_HASH_LISTPACK (16) — small hash, listpack payload
       * RDB_TYPE_ZSET_LISTPACK (17) — small zset, listpack payload
       * RDB_TYPE_LIST_QUICKLIST_2 (18) — list, quicklist of
         listpacks (upstream ALWAYS uses this for lists, regardless
         of size — so the test will be unable to seed any list at
         all until this lands)
       * RDB_TYPE_SET_LISTPACK (20) — small set, listpack payload
     Acceptance is the inverse of the f3dfe89 narrowing: drop the
     CONFIG SET *=0 block in live_redis_rdb_corpus_decodes_cleanly,
     re-add list_small/set_intset/set_listpack/hash_lp/zset_lp to
     expected_keys, and verify the harvest decodes cleanly.

  2. Pure rustfmt deltas inside the same test:
       * collapse send_owned's signature back onto one line where
         rustfmt's column budget allows;
       * collapse the `let reply = read_resp_frame_from_stream(conn)
         .unwrap_or_else(...);` chain back to a single wrapped form.
     No behavioral change.

Note: three .beads/beads.db*.{corrupt,backup}_orphan_recovery_*
sidecar files are still untracked in the working tree — these are
the ephemeral byproducts of br's orphan-recovery routine (see
hs8w in git history for context). They are intentionally not
staged here. Operator hasn't authorized deletion, so they stay
on disk and out of the index.

files.patch:
@@ -229,6 +229,7 @@
 {"id":"frankenredis-ajk","title":"Implement real XINFO CONSUMERS pending count and idle/inactive time","description":"## Problem

fr-store/src/lib.rs line 5796: xinfo_consumers returns Vec<Vec<u8>> (just consumer names).
fr-command/src/lib.rs lines 4169-4179: stream_consumer_info_to_frame hardcodes pending=0, idle=0, inactive=0.

## What Redis Returns

XINFO CONSUMERS key group returns per-consumer:
- name: consumer name
- pending: number of PEL entries owned by this consumer
- idle: milliseconds since last interaction (XREADGROUP/XCLAIM/XACK)
- inactive: milliseconds since last successful XREADGROUP (Redis 7.2+)

## Implementation Plan

1. Change Store::xinfo_consumers to return Vec<(Vec<u8>, usize, u64, u64)> — (name, pending_count, last_interaction_ms, last_read_ms)
2. Compute pending_count by scanning the PEL and counting entries where consumer matches
3. Track last_interaction_ms per consumer in StreamGroupState (update on XREADGROUP, XCLAIM, XACK)
4. Track last_read_ms per consumer (update only on XREADGROUP with >)
5. Update stream_consumer_info_to_frame to use real values

## Files to Modify

- crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs: StreamGroupState consumer tracking, xinfo_consumers
- crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs: stream_consumer_info_to_frame","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:09:21.561101730Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:54:44.406972571Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:54:44.406145205Z","close_reason":"XINFO CONSUMERS now returns real pending count and idle time computed from PEL, not hardcoded zeros","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenredis-ajk","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-7o5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:09:31.307893656Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"frankenredis-an8k","title":"Expand fuzz corpus with diverse seed inputs","notes":"Expanded: RDB 3→14 seeds, AOF 5→14 seeds, DUMP 2→5 seeds covering all data types","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-17T01:32:13.335629864Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-17T01:36:22.635690544Z","closed_at":"2026-04-17T01:36:22.635690544Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-ao1i","title":"[HIGH] COPY src==dst returns 0/1 instead of error + RESTORE busy-key error precedence","description":"Surfaced by live_redis_core_copy_matches_runtime: copy_src_eq_dst_no_replace + copy_src_eq_dst_with_replace — upstream 'ERR source and destination objects are the same', ours returns Integer(0)/Integer(1) as if copy succeeded. restore_bad_payload_existing_key — our error-check order gives 'DUMP payload version or checksum are wrong' before BUSYKEY; upstream checks existence first. Fix in fr-command COPY (add src-vs-dst guard) + RESTORE (move BUSYKEY check before payload validation).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T07:20:18.874206188Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T22:51:49.101679686Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T22:51:49.099024919Z","close_reason":"Already resolved: live_redis_core_copy_matches_runtime passes 100% under STRICT mode (COPY src==dst and RESTORE BUSYKEY precedence fixes landed previously).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankenredis-aqgx","title":"[HIGH] fr-persist::decode_rdb: support upstream compact encoding type tags 11/16/17/18/20","description":"Surfaced by br-frankenredis-8w1e RDB corpus harvest test (live_redis_rdb_corpus_decodes_cleanly): when upstream encodes small data structures, it uses compact-form RDB type bytes that fr-persist::decode_rdb does not recognize today. Match table:

* RDB_TYPE_SET_INTSET (11) — all-integer set, intset payload
* RDB_TYPE_HASH_LISTPACK (16) — small hash, listpack payload
* RDB_TYPE_ZSET_LISTPACK (17) — small zset, listpack payload
* RDB_TYPE_LIST_QUICKLIST_2 (18) — list, quicklist of listpacks (upstream ALWAYS uses this for lists, regardless of size)
* RDB_TYPE_SET_LISTPACK (20) — small set, listpack payload

Without these, fr-persist returns InvalidFrame on any upstream-produced dump.rdb that contains a list of any size, OR small sets/hashes/zsets that haven't been pinned past their listpack thresholds. The br-frankenredis-8w1e test pins thresholds to 0 to bypass; lifting that pin requires implementing these decoders.

Listpack and intset decoders already exist in fr-store (used for DUMP/RESTORE). The fix is mostly wiring fr-store helpers into fr-persist::decode_rdb, plus a quicklist envelope decoder for type 18.

Acceptance: re-enable compact encoding pins in live_redis_rdb_corpus_decodes_cleanly (drop the CONFIG SET *=0 block) and add the corresponding shapes back to expected_keys; harvest runs through with all 11+ shapes decoding cleanly.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-28T04:05:18.506584651Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-28T04:05:18.506584651Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-as1","title":"Wire CONFIG SET maxclients to fr-server connection acceptance","description":"## Problem

fr-server/src/main.rs line 33 defines `const MAX_CLIENTS: usize = 10_000` and uses it at line 499 to reject new connections. CONFIG SET maxclients updates the config table in fr-runtime but never propagates to fr-server's actual enforcement.

## What Redis Does

Redis's CONFIG SET maxclients immediately changes the rlimit and connection acceptance threshold. New connections beyond the limit get an error response and are closed.

## Implementation Plan

1. Add a `max_clients` field to `ServerState` in fr-runtime (initialized to 10000)
2. In `handle_config_set`, when maxclients is set, update `self.server.max_clients`
3. In fr-server's main loop, read `runtime.server.max_clients` (or expose via a getter) instead of the const
4. Also update `store.server_maxclients` so INFO reports the correct value
5. Remove the `MAX_CLIENTS` const from fr-server

## Files to Modify

- `crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs`: Add field to ServerState, handle in config_set
- `crates/fr-server/src/main.rs`: Replace const with runtime read
- `crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs`: Already has server_maxclients field (added in f8c5a8a)

## Testing

- Unit test: CONFIG SET maxclients 100 → CONFIG GET maxclients returns 100
- Unit test: INFO clients section reports the new maxclients value
- Integration: verify connection rejection beyond the configured limit (may need fr-conformance fixture)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:25.451789990Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:42:12.846797806Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:42:12.845683417Z","close_reason":"maxclients wired: ServerState.max_clients + CONFIG SET/GET + fr-server reads runtime value","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenredis-as1","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-kgn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:28.584373097Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"frankenredis-atzq","title":"redis parity: fr-command wrong-subcommand arity wording should use command text","description":"Evidence: vendored Redis returns command wording for CONFIG RESETSTAT, OBJECT FREQ/IDLETIME, CLIENT GETREDIR/PAUSE, ACL SETUSER/HELP/WHOAMI, XGROUP CREATE, and XINFO STREAM arity failures. Local fr-command still special-cases WrongSubcommandArity::to_resp to emit subcommand wording for several families, and stale unit expectations in lib.rs/lua_eval.rs preserved the wrong oracle text.

Current gap: crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs mismatched the oracle on wrong-subcommand arity text, leaving cargo test -p fr-command red.

Real impl path: simplify WrongSubcommandArity formatting to Redis command wording and refresh the stale unit expectations in crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs and crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:14:13.408576035Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:22:11.870741770Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:22:11.870741770Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-auzj","title":"redis parity: CLIENT NO-EVICT/NO-TOUCH/SETINFO/GETREDIR/TRACKINGINFO should reject script context","description":"Vendored Redis marks CLIENT NO-EVICT, CLIENT NO-TOUCH, CLIENT SETINFO, CLIENT GETREDIR, and CLIENT TRACKINGINFO as NOSCRIPT in legacy_redis_code/redis/src/commands/client-no-evict.json, client-no-touch.json, client-setinfo.json, client-getredir.json, and client-trackinginfo.json. The client_cmd branches at crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:11133, 11144, 11328, and 11335 currently execute without any script_nesting_level guard. Add Redis-compatible rejection after arity validation for each subcommand and cover them from Lua.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T04:07:28.670276364Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T04:26:03.340056210Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T04:26:03.337548762Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
